questions.text: A two-meter-high tank is full of water. A hole is made in the middle of the tank. The speed of efflux is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The following is the solution: At the middle of the tank, the height of the water is 1m, So efflux velocity=V=√2gh =√2x9.8x1=√19.36 =4.4 m/s

questions.explanation: The following is the solution: At the middle of the tank, the height of the water is 1m, So efflux velocity=V=√2gh =√2x9.8x1=√19.36 =4.4 m/s
hints: hole is made in the middle

questions.text: Water flows from a 6.0cm diameter pipe into an 8.0cm diameter pipe. The speed in the 6.0cm pipe is 5.0m/s. The speed in the 8cm pipe is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The following is the solution: D1=6cm and D2=8cm or R1=3cm and R2=4cm, V1=5m/s , So by A1v1=A2V2 or r12v1=r22v2 =32x 5= 42 x v2 v2=45/16 =2.8m/s

questions.explanation: The following is the solution: D1=6cm and D2=8cm or R1=3cm and R2=4cm, V1=5m/s , so by A1v1=A2V2 or r12v1=r22v2 =32x 5=42 x v2 v2=45/16 =2.8m/s

questions.text: A metal sphere of radius r is dropped into a tank of water. As it sinks at speed v, it experiences a drag force F given by F=krv, where k is a constant. What are the SI base units of k?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The following is the solution: F=krv k=F/rv =ma/rv =kgm-1s-1

questions.explanation: The following is the solution: F=krv k=F/rv =ma/rv =kgm-1s-1
hints: dropped into a tank of water

questions.text: Eight drops of water each radius 2mm are falling through the air at a terminal velocity of 8cm/s. If they coalesce to form a single drop, the terminal velocity of the combined drop will be:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The following is the solution:Vt' =n2/3Vt, Here n=no. of drops. Thus Vt'=82/3Vt =4x8=32cm/s

questions.explanation: The following is the solution: Vt'=n2/3Vt, Here n=no. of drops. Thus Vt'=82/3Vt=4x8=32cm/s

questions.text: The acceleration of a falling body in a fluid depends upon:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The acceleration of a falling body in a fluid is influenced by its velocity. As a body falls, its velocity increases, and the drag force from the fluid also increases. This results in a change in acceleration over time as the body approaches its terminal velocity, where the drag force equals the force of gravity.

The viscosity of the fluid plays a significant role in the acceleration of a falling body. More viscous fluids, like honey, provide more resistance to motion, resulting in a different acceleration profile compared to less viscous fluids, such as air.

The density of the falling body affects its buoyancy in the fluid, which, in turn, affects the net force acting on the body. The acceleration of the body in the fluid depends on this net force, which is influenced by the density of the body.

The statement "All of the above" means that the acceleration of a falling body in a fluid is influenced by all these factors together, and no single factor alone can fully explain the complex dynamics of a body falling through a fluid.

questions.explanation: The statement "All of the above" means that the acceleration of a falling body in a fluid is influenced by all these factors together, and no single factor alone can fully explain the complex dynamics of a body falling through a fluid. Acceleration depends on velocity, density, radius, and viscosity.

questions.text: A fluid is undergoing incompressible flow which represents that:
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: Pressure at a given point cannot change with time
Velocity at given point cannot change with time
The density cannot change change with time or location
The velocity must be the same everywhere

questions.explanation: Incompressible means that liquid will not compress and density will remain constant

questions.text: A larger water tank open at the top has a small hole in the bottom when the water level is 30m above the bottom of the tank, the speed of the water leaking from the hole is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The following is the solution: It can be solved by Torricelli Theorem; As speed is given by; V=√2gh =√2x10x30 =24.49

options.text: Cannot be calculated unless the area of the hole is given

questions.explanation: The following is the solution: It can be solved by Torricelli Theorem; As speed is given by; V=√2gh =√2x10x30 =24.49
hints: larger water tank open at the top
a small hole in the bottom
above the bottom of the tank

questions.text: Bermoulli's equation can be derived from the conservation of:
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: Work=K.E+P.E it is the first step in derivation of bernouli equation, so Bernoulli's equation can be derived from the conservation of energy

questions.text: One end of the cylindrical pipe has a radius of 1.5cm. If water streams (density=1.0x103kg/m3) steadily out of it at 7.0m/s, the volume rate is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The following is the solution: Volume rate: V/t=A v=(π r2) Velocity=3.14x(0.015)2 x 7 =4.9x10-3 m3/s

questions.explanation: The following is the solution: Volume rate: V/t=A v=(π r2) Velocity=3.14x(0.015)2 x 7 =4.9x10-3 m3/s
hints: One end of the cylindrical pipe

questions.text: An incompressible liquid flows along the pipe with the area of cross-section A1 and A2 with velocities V1 and V2 respectively. The ratio of the speeds V1/V2 is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The following is the solution: It is the equation of continuity; As; A1V1=A2V2 , Hence, V1/V2=A2/A1

questions.explanation: The following is the solution: It is the equation of continuity; As; A1V1=A2V2 , Hence, V1/V2=A2/A1
